articles.author: GLORIA COLEMAN The News-Review
articles.description: It took him a little bit to make up his mind but Days Creek Charter School Superintendent Joe LaFountaine decided to retire again after 42 years working in Oregon schools.
articles.title: Days Creek Charter School's LaFountaine looks toward returning to retirement

articles.description: The Rose Elementary School campus in downtown Roseburg was alive Monday afternoon with the sounds of children and their families exploring the world of rhythm, sounds and meaningful play.
articles.title: Roseburg families engage in learning through rhythm, rhyme and sound

articles.author: GLORIA COLEMAN The News-Review
articles.description: A new agreement will allow Umpqua Community College students to pursue a career in the pharmacy through a partnership with Pacific University.
articles.title: Umpqua, Chemeketa community colleges initiate pathway with Pacific University for pharmacy degree

articles.author: The News-Review staff
articles.description: WINSTON — A drinking water warning is in effect for the next 24 hours in Winston following a loss of water pressure in the distribution system Monday. A notice from the Winston Dillard Water District states potentially harmful bacteria could…
articles.title: Drinking water warning in effect in Winston

articles.author: GLORIA COLEMAN The News-Review
articles.description: CANYONVILLE — Abacela Winery, HillCrest Winery, Trella Vineyards and Freed Estate Winery came away with awards Saturday night when the Umpqua Valley Winegrowers Association held the Greatest of the Grape at Seven Feathers Casino Resort in Canyonville.
articles.title: Abacela Winery's 2025 Grenache Rosé is the Greatest of the Grape

articles.author: ANDREW GRIFFIN The News-Review
articles.description: For over 30 years, Patrick Hull has served in the health care industry, providing leadership and management to health care projects and organizations across the country. This month, he has retired from his most recent position: executive director of the…
articles.title: Roseburg VA Health Care System Executive Director Patrick Hull retires

articles.author: Jim Teece
articles.description:
Business Oregon’s Credit Enhancement Fund (”CEF”) program. CEF is a loan guarantee program that provides insurance for up to 80% of a commercial loan to help businesses get access to capital for efforts like: commercial real estate and equipment acquisition, day-to-day working capital financing (accounts receivable and inventory), and commercial construction. Business Oregon has announced that they have lowered most of…
The post Business Oregon’s Credit Enhancement Fund becomes easier to access appeared first on Southern Oregon Business Journal.
articles.title: Business Oregon’s Credit Enhancement Fund becomes easier to access

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg police reported arrests and citations in the March 12 log, including DUII, theft, warrant arrests, prohibited camping, a drug case, a crash with injuries, trespass and city ordinance violations. The published log also included several early March 13 incidents.
articles.title: Police Log: March 12, 2026

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg police reported multiple arrests and citations on today’s log page, including warrant arrests, drinking in public, camping violations, trespassing, meth-related charges, a DUII case, and a theft arrest tied to a stolen trailer.
articles.title: Police Log: March 11, 2026

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: A kitten stolen earlier this week from FCAT Rescue & Thrift Shop in Winston was safely returned Thursday, and a woman was arrested after surveillance video helped identify the suspect, according to the rescue’s founder.
articles.title: Kitten Returned After Theft From Winston Rescue, Woman Arrested

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: A $450,000 state funding allocation will support renovations at the Glide Revitalization Center, expanding access to child care, community space, library resources, social services and other programs intended to serve rural residents in and around Glide.
articles.title: $450,000 Allocated To Support Glide Revitalization Center Project

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Cow Creek Umpqua Tribe opened a new community pharmacy Wednesday in Roseburg, expanding local access to prescription services and integrated care for Tribal citizens and other patients through Cow Creek Health & Wellness clinics.
articles.title: Cow Creek Umpqua Tribe Opens New Pharmacy in Roseburg, Expanding Local Access to Care

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Families across Douglas County are invited to the 3rd Annual Shamrock Quest on Saturday, March 14, from noon to 3 p.m. at Aviva Health in Roseburg. The free event will feature games, activities, food trucks, prizes and community resource booths.
articles.title: 3rd Annual Shamrock Quest Set For March 14 In Roseburg

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg police said a 36-year-old woman was jailed Tuesday night after allegedly taking seven puppies and their mother from a camp near West Stanton Street. Officers said the dogs were returned to their owner, and the woman was booked on eight counts of first-degree theft.
articles.title: Roseburg Woman Jailed After Police Say She Took Seven Puppies And Their Mother From Camp

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg police said a 36-year-old man was jailed Tuesday after officers found him in a reported stolen Ford F-150 in a Safeway parking lot. Police said he admitted taking the truck but claimed he had permission from its previous owner.
articles.title: Roseburg Man Jailed After Police Recover Reported Stolen Pickup

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: Roseburg Police’s March 10 log included a recovered stolen truck, a DUII case, camping and burn citations, a trespass arrest, and a theft case involving seven puppies and their mother, according to the daily police log.
articles.title: Police Log: March 10, 2026

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: The Oregon Naturalist Southwest Oregon field experience will take participants across the Umpqua Basin and Klamath-Siskiyou region from April through September, offering expert-led excursions focused on ecology, wildlife, geology and natural history.
articles.title: Oregon Naturalist Field Program To Explore Southwest Oregon Landscapes

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: March is Red Cross Month, and the American Red Cross is encouraging people in Southwest Oregon to volunteer. Open roles include disaster response, shelter support, blood donor ambassador and home fire campaign team member positions.
articles.title: American Red Cross Highlights Volunteer Opportunities During Red Cross Month

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: A community garden work day is planned for Friday, March 13, in Roseburg. Volunteers are invited to help restore the garden from 3 p.m. to 6 p.m. at 1450 SE Mill St. Organizers said tools and supplies will be provided, and all ages and abilities are welcome.
articles.title: Community Garden Work Day Planned March 13 In Roseburg

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: A judge upheld $77,767 in penalties against the Winchester Water Control District over water quality violations tied to 2023 repair work at Winchester Dam on the North Umpqua River, according to a final order issued March 6.
articles.title: Judge Upholds $77,767 In Winchester Dam Repair Penalties

articles.author: Joseph Ziegler
articles.description: A Roseburg man faces multiple charges after authorities say he fired at vehicles on Interstate 5 Saturday night before leading police into Winston, where deputies used a spike strip to stop him. One driver reported a bullet shattered his truck’s back window.
articles.title: Roseburg man accused of firing at vehicles on Interstate 5, leading deputies on pursuit into Winston

articles.description: Roseburg police reported six incidents Monday, including a theft, camping violations, a parole violation, an illegal burn, and a traffic crash involving a suspended driver, according to the City of Roseburg Police Logs.
